examples.title: Brute Force SAT Checker
examples.description: Naive exponential algorithm to check if a CNF formula is satisfiable
examples.explanation: This brute force SAT solver tries all possible variable assignments. It works for small n but becomes impractical quickly due to exponential growth.

examples.title: Polynomial Time Reduction Template
examples.description: Generic structure for polynomial-time reduction from problem A to problem B
examples.explanation: This template shows how to reduce problem A to problem B in polynomial time. If we can solve B efficiently and perform the transformation efficiently, then A is also efficiently solvable.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n^c) where c is constant for transformation

examples.title: Pseudopolynomial Knapsack DP
examples.description: Dynamic programming solution for 0/1 Knapsack - pseudopolynomial in input size
examples.explanation: This DP solution for knapsack runs in O(n×W) time. The complexity depends on the weight capacity W (numeric value), not just the input length. If W is given in binary, the input size is O(log W), making this exponential in input size.
